source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Lifting Device, Ensco 8502

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Green Canyon Block 237

event.top_event: Loss of vessel position control and Emergency Disconnect System activation
actions_taken: Emergency Disconnect System (EDS) activated from drill floor
Riser displaced immediately with sea water
ROV inspection performed to verify well integrity
Joint investigation by BSEE and USCG initiated
event.summary: On 16-OCT-2014 at 2250 hours, a blown fuse on the #4 generator aboard the Ensco 8502 caused loss of four of eight thrusters. Unable to maintain position, the vessel drifted toward the designated 'Red Zone' approximately 82 feet from well center. The Driller activated the EDS, which functioned as designed, shearing drill pipe and closing the Annular before unlatching the LMRP. Approximately 150 barrels of SBM were released from the riser and choke/kill hoses. No injuries occurred. The well remained secure and undamaged.
recommendations: No recommendations issued by Houma District BSEE at this time

hazards.name: Electrical system failure on drilling vessel

threats.name: Loss of four thrusters due to power loss
Risk of riser and BOP stack damage

consequences.name: Release of 150 barrels of synthetic-based mud
Environmental pollution to Gulf of Mexico waters
Potential wellbore and subsea equipment damage

controls.name: Redundant thruster system (8 thrusters total)
controls.barrier_role: Maintain vessel position and prevent drift

supporting_text: a blown fuse on the #4 generator caused a series of events throughout the ship's electrical system which resulted in a temporary loss of four of the vessel's eight thrusters
The vessel, now only operating with half its thrusters, was unable to maintain its position due to the currents

controls.name: Red Zone designation and monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Warn crew of excessive vessel drift toward wellhead
controls.lod_basis: Operational procedure to alert crew before critical distance reached

supporting_text: The 'Red Zone' is an area designated by the operator to give a warning when the ship is too far off course and in danger of damaging drilling equipment subsea
Once the 'Red Zone' was reached, approximately 3 minutes after the blown fuse, the Driller onboard the rig activated the EDS from the drill floor

controls.name: Emergency Disconnect System (EDS)
controls.barrier_role: Disconnect riser and seal wellbore to prevent fluid loss during uncontrolled drift
controls.lod_basis: Automated safety system designed to isolate well and allow safe vessel drift

supporting_text: Upon activating the EDS, the system is designed to shear any pipe across the stack and close the Annular before unlatching the Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP) from the BOP
This process should enable the rig to secure the fluid in the riser via the closed Annular and seal off the wellbore via the Blind Shear Rams (BSRs)
The EDS system worked as designed, and the crew regained control of the vessel approximately 519 feet away from the ships original position

controls.name: Blind Shear Rams (BSRs)
controls.barrier_role: Seal wellbore and prevent fluid release during riser disconnect
controls.lod_basis: Component of EDS designed to isolate wellbore

supporting_text: seal off the wellbore via the Blind Shear Rams (BSRs)
ROV footage showed that the well was secure and undamaged

controls.name: Riser displacement with seawater
controls.barrier_role: Prevent further release of SBM after initial disconnect
controls.lod_basis: Post-incident response procedure to minimize environmental impact

supporting_text: The Riser was displaced immediately with sea water to prevent any further release of SBM into offshore waters

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
